Table NameEXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_PSTN_PRTCN_ARRNGMNT_ASSGNMNT
Functional NameExchange tradable derivative position Protection arrangement assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionExchange tradable derivative position Protection arrangement assignment is the combination of Exchange tradable derivative position and Protection arrangement   stating which Exchange tradable derivative position is offering protection for which Protection arrangement.
Notes
Number Of Columns14
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6BYR_ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
7SLLR_ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
8PRTCTN_ARRNGMNT_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
9PRTCTN_ARRNGMNT_RL_TYPPFYString (1) DOMProtection arrangement role type (Input layer)
10BYR_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
11SLLR_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
12ALLCTD_UNSD_CLLTRL_VLYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
13EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_PRTCN_ARRNGMNT_ASSGNMNT_TYPYString (1) DOMExchange tradable derivative Protection arrangement assignment type
14PRTCTN_ALLCTD_VLYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_IDThe security identifier of the exchange tradable derivative.
6BYR_ENTTY_RL_TYPThe buyer party role.
7SLLR_ENTTY_RL_TYPThe seller role.
8PRTCTN_ARRNGMNT_IDAn identifier to univocally identify each protection arragement.
9PRTCTN_ARRNGMNT_RL_TYPProtection arrangement role type distinguishes the Protection arrangement role according to their nature.
12ALLCTD_UNSD_CLLTRL_VLAllocated unused collateral value is an amount that expresses the protection value that is not used for risk mitigation and, for FINREP reporting purposes is allocated to a specific Loan and advance, which is the Long non-negotiable security position.
13EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_PRTCN_ARRNGMNT_ASSGNMNT_TYPExchange tradable derivative Protection arrangement assignment type distinguishes Exchange tradable derivative Protection arrangement assignment according to the direction, given or received.
14PRTCTN_ALLCTD_VLProtection allocated value is the maximum amount of the protection value, via the Protection arrangement, that can be considered as credit protection for the Long security position. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.

10EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_PSTN_RL_TYPPYString (2) DOMExchange tradable derivative position role type (Input Layer)
11ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTNYString (3) DOMAccounting classification (Input Layer)
12ASST_ENCMBRNC_SRC_INDCTRYStringDOMAsset encumbrance source indicator (Input Layer)
13BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_ASST_PSTN_ACCNTNG_STNDRD_TYPYString (2) DOMBalance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position by accounting standard (Input layer)
14BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_ASST_PSTN_TKN_PSSSSN_TYPYString (2) DOMBalance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position taken into possession type (Input Layer)
15CST_BSD_MTHD_LCM_INDCTRYString (1) DOMHedge accounting - At cost based method or Lower of cost or market (LOCOM) indicator (Input layer)
16ENCMBRD_ASST_INDCTRYStringDOMEncumbered asset indicator (Input Layer)
17FDCRYYString (1) DOMFiduciary asset indicator (Input layer)
18FV_HRRCHYYString (1) DOMFair value hierarchy (Input Layer)
19PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYPYString (1) DOMPrudential portfolio type (Input Layer)
20TYP_HDGYString (2) DOMType of hedge (Input layer)
21ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FVDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
22ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FV_EXPSR_CRDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
23CRRYNG_AMNTDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
24DRCGNSD_EXPSR_ACCMLTD_IMPRMNTDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
25FVDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
26FV_CHNGDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
27GCA_EXPSR_DRCGNSD_EXCHNG_CLLTRLDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
28GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNT_INTL_RCGNTNDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
29TKN_PSSSSN_DTDateDOMAll possible dates
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1SLLR_PRTY_IDThe party identifier of the involved seller.
2SLLR_ENTTY_RL_TYPThe seller role.
3DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
4RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
5BYR_PRTY_IDThe party identifier of the involved buyer.
6BYR_ENTTY_RL_TYPThe buyer party role.
7EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_SCRTY_IDThe security identifier of the exchange tradable derivative.
8ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
11ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTNAccounting portfolio where the asset or liability is recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP –under Regulation (EU) 2015/534 (ECB/2015/13) applied by the observed agent's legal entity.
12ASST_ENCMBRNC_SRC_INDCTRAsset encumbrance source indicator signals whether the liabity or off-balance sheet item is a source of encumbrance for an asset or not.
13BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_ASST_PSTN_ACCNTNG_STNDRD_TYPBalance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position by accounting standard distinguishes the Balance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position by the applied accounting standard.
14BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_ASST_PSTN_TKN_PSSSSN_TYPBalance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position obtained by taking possession type is a dsicriminant that distinguishes between Balance sheet recognised exchange tradable derivative asset position obtained by taking possession and not obtained by taking possession
15CST_BSD_MTHD_LCM_INDCTRAt cost based method or Lower of costs or market (LOCOM) indicator distinguishes the used accounting portfolio for national General Accepted Accounting Principles (nGAAP) between those that use the At cost based method or Lower of costs or market (LOCOM), and those that do not use At cost based method or Lower of costs or carket (LOCOM).
16ENCMBRD_ASST_INDCTREncumbered asset indicator signals whether the asset is encumbered or not.
17FDCRYIdentification of instruments in which the observed agent acts in its own name but on behalf of and with the risk borne by a third party.
18FV_HRRCHYLevel of fair value of a financial asset or liability according to the hierarchy defined in IFRS 13. Applicable to national GAAP based on BAD only if the allocation of instruments is measured at fair value according to different levels.
19PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between trading and banking (or non-trading) book.
20TYP_HDGType of hedge applicable under the relevant accounting standard.
21ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FVAmount of gains and losses from re-measurement of the asset accumulated from the initial recognition to the reference date.
22ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FV_EXPSR_CRAccumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014 of the exposure that is derecognised because of the asset obtained by taking possession.

Note: This amount must only be reported when it is negative.
23CRRYNG_AMNTThe carrying amount in accordance with Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
24DRCGNSD_EXPSR_ACCMLTD_IMPRMNTAccumulated impairment is the amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the derecognised exposure on the reporting reference date for which the asset obtained by taking possession has been obtained.

This data attribute applies to assets subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.
25FVFair value as defined in IFRS 13.9.
26FV_CHNGFair value changes in the period and before taxes.
28GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNT_INTL_RCGNTNGross carrying amount at the point in time of the initial recognition in the balance sheet 
29TKN_PSSSSN_DTBalance sheet recognition date is a date on the asset obtained by taking possession  was taken into possession and thus recognised on the balance sheet.

Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
10 nGAAP: Loans and receivables
12nGAAP: Held-to-maturity investments
14IFRS: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with IFRS.
2IFRS: Financial assets held for trading: Financial assets held for trading in accordance with IFRS.
21IFRS: Financial liabilities measured at amortised cost: Financial liabilities measured at amortised cost in accordance with IFRS
23IFRS: Financial liabilities held for trading: Financial liabilities held for trading in accordance with IFRS
25IFRS: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with IFRS
3nGAAP: Trading Financial assets: Trading financial assets in accordance with national GAAP.
31nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial liabilities measured at a cost-based method: Non-trading non-derivative financial liabilities measured at a cost-based method accordance with national GAAP based on BAD
33nGAAP: Trading financial liabilities: Trading financial liabilities in accordance with national GAAP based on BAD
35nGAAP: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with nationa GAAP based on BAD
4IFRS: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets measured at fair value through profit and loss and designated as such upon initial recognition or subsequently in accordance with IFRS, except those classified as financial assets held for trading.
41IFRS: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with IFRS.
45nGAAP: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with national GAAP.
46nGAAP: Financial assets held for trading
47nGAAP: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with national GAAP.
48nGAAP: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss
5nGAAP: Available-for-sale financial assets
6IFRS: Financial assets at amortised cost: Financial assets measured at amortised cost in accordance with IFRS.
64nGAAP: financial assets at fair value or strict LOCOM
7nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.
711Accounting portfolios for financial assets other than classified as held for sale, excluding financial assets held for trading, trading financial assets and cash and cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits
73nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. LOCOM: nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets at LOCOM
74nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. Other than LOCOM
76nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. LOCOM: nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method at LOCOM
77nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. Other than LOCOM
8IFRS: Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income: Financial assets measured at fair value through other comprehensive income due to business model and cash-flows characteristics in accordance with IFRS.
83Investments in subsidiaries, joint ventures and associates.
851nGAAP: Accounting portfolios for trading financial assetss. Cost based method or LOCOM
852nGAAP: Accounting portfolios for trading financial liabilities. Cost based method or LOCOM
9nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.
90Under IFRS 9 impairment: Off balance sheet accounting classification: under IFRS 9 impairment
911Measured under IAS 37: Off balance sheet accounting classification: measured under IAS 37
912Measured under IFRS 4: Off balance sheet accounting classification: measured under IFRS 4
92Measured at fair value through profit or loss: Off balance sheet accounting classification: IFRS 9 fair valued commitments and financial guarantees
93Under nGAAP: Off balance sheet accounting classification: measured under nGAAP based on BAD

FV_HRRCHY
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Level 1: Level 1 inputs used for the measurement of fair value. Level 1 inputs are quoted prices in active markets for identical assets or liabilities that the entity can access at the measurement date
2Level 2: Level 2 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 2 inputs are inputs other than quoted prices included within Level 1 that are observable for the asset or liability, either directly or indirectly
3Level 3: Level 3 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 3 inputs are unobservable inputs for the asset or liability
PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYP
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Trading book
2Non-trading book
TYP_HDG
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Fair value hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (a)
11Hedges other than cash flow hedge and hedge of net investment in a foreign operation: Economic hedge
12Economic hedge. With use of fair value option
13Economic hedge. Other
2Cash flow hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (b)
3Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation: Hedging relationship as defined in IFRS 9.6.5.2 (c)
4Portfolio fair value hedges of interest rate risk: Hedging relationship as defined in IAS39.AG 114-132
5Portfolio cash flow hedges of interest rate risk: Hedging relationship as defined in IAS39.AG 114-132
6Cost-price hedge: Hedging relationship as defined in Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.114. This hedging relationship is applicable only under nGAAP based on BAD
7Other than Fair value hedge, Cash flow hedge, Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation, Portfolio fair value hedges of interest rate risk, Portfolio cash flow hedges of interest rate risk, Cost-price hedge: Other applicable hedging relationship for nGAAP based on BAD reporters

Table NameFNDMNTL_RVW_TRDNG_BK_STNDRD_APPRCH_RSK_MSR_ETD_PSTNS
Functional NameFundamental review of the trading book standard approach risk measure for ETD positions
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA measured risk of an ETD position against a particular risk factor
Notes
Number Of Columns22
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1FNDMNTL_RVW_TRDNG_BK_STNDRD_APPRCH_RSK_MSR_IDPYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_SCRTY_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
4RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
6ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
7SLLR_ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
8BYR_ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
9SLLR_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
10BYR_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
11RSK_FCTR_IDFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
12CRRNCY_RCRDYString (3) DOMSubdomain for domain ISO-4217 Currency Codes (ISO4217 Domain) (Input Layer)
13RSK_MSR_TYPYString (1) DOMRisk measure type
14RSK_MSR_TYP_PSTNYString (1) DOMRisk measure type by position
15SHCK_DRCTNYString (1) DOMShock direction
16CRVTR_RSK_WGHTDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
17CRVTR_VLTN_DFFRNCDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
18DLT_SNSTVTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
19OPTN_MTRTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
20TNRDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
21UNDRLYNG_RSDL_MTRTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
22VG_SNSTVTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
2EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_SCRTY_IDThe security identifier of the exchange tradable derivative.
3DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
4RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
7SLLR_ENTTY_RL_TYPThe buyer party role.
8BYR_ENTTY_RL_TYPThe seller role.
10BYR_PRTY_IDThe party identifier of the involved buyer.
11RSK_FCTR_IDRisk factor identifier is a number that uniquely identifies the Risk factor.
12CRRNCY_RCRDCurrency of record is the currency in which the curvature risk is measured.
13RSK_MSR_TYPRisk measure type distinguishes Fundamental review of the trading book standard approach risk measure according to its nature.
14RSK_MSR_TYP_PSTNA discriminator to distinguish between different types of risk measures by position.
15SHCK_DRCTNThe direction of the change in uderlying (up or down), for each non-linear position we will have an upshift and a downshift measure recorded.
16CRVTR_RSK_WGHTThe weight associated with the curvature.
17CRVTR_VLTN_DFFRNCCurvature recorded as a difference between the normal valuation and  a revaluation after a small  increase OR decrease in an underyling risk factor, this is an amount interprted in the curency of record, e.g. 100 dollars. We only use carvature for non-linear instruments (e.g. options).
18DLT_SNSTVTYAn amount interpreted in the currency of the record, e.g., 100 dollars.
19OPTN_MTRTYThe option maturity to which this sensitivity relates.
20TNRThe tenor point of the related curve if this is a risk to a curve based risk factor.
21UNDRLYNG_RSDL_MTRTYWhere required, the underlying residual maturity to which the sensitivity is wwa created. Required when we consider that the sensitivity came from a change in a volatility surface.
22VG_SNSTVTYThe sensitivity, as an amount (e.g. 100 dollars).

Table NameFNDMNTL_RVW_TRDNG_BK_STNDRD_APPRCH_RSK_MSR_FR_SCRTY_PSTNS
Functional NameFundamental review of the trading book standard approach risk measure for security positions
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA measured risk of a Security position against a particular risk factor
Notes
Number Of Columns20
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1FNDMNTL_RVW_TRDNG_BK_STNDRD_APPRCH_RSK_MSR_IDPYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4INVSTR_ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
5DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
6ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
7SCRTY_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8INVSTR_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
9RSK_FCTR_IDFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
10CRRNCY_RCRDYString (3) DOMSubdomain for domain ISO-4217 Currency Codes (ISO4217 Domain) (Input Layer)
11RSK_MSR_TYPYString (1) DOMRisk measure type
12RSK_MSR_TYP_PSTNYString (1) DOMRisk measure type by position
13SHCK_DRCTNYString (1) DOMShock direction
14CRVTR_RSK_WGHTDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
15CRVTR_VLTN_DFFRNCDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
16DLT_SNSTVTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
17OPTN_MTRTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
18TNRDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
19UNDRLYNG_RSDL_MTRTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
20VG_SNSTVTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
4INVSTR_ENTTY_RL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
5DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
7SCRTY_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
9RSK_FCTR_IDRisk factor identifier is a number that uniquely identifies the Risk factor.
10CRRNCY_RCRDCurrency of record is the currency in which the curvature risk is measured.
11RSK_MSR_TYPRisk measure type distinguishes Fundamental review of the trading book standard approach risk measure according to its nature.
12RSK_MSR_TYP_PSTNA discriminator to distinguish between different types of risk measures by position.
13SHCK_DRCTNThe direction of the change in uderlying (up or down), for each non-linear position we will have an upshift and a downshift measure recorded.
14CRVTR_RSK_WGHTThe weight associated with the curvature.
15CRVTR_VLTN_DFFRNCCurvature recorded as a difference between the normal valuation and  a revaluation after a small  increase OR decrease in an underyling risk factor, this is an amount interprted in the curency of record, e.g. 100 dollars. We only use carvature for non-linear instruments (e.g. options).
16DLT_SNSTVTYAn amount interpreted in the currency of the record, e.g., 100 dollars.
17OPTN_MTRTYThe option maturity to which this sensitivity relates.
18TNRThe tenor point of the related curve if this is a risk to a curve based risk factor.
19UNDRLYNG_RSDL_MTRTYWhere required, the underlying residual maturity to which the sensitivity is wwa created. Required when we consider that the sensitivity came from a change in a volatility surface.
20VG_SNSTVTYThe sensitivity, as an amount (e.g. 100 dollars).
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort
Order

Table NameFNDMNTL_RVW_TRDNG_BK_STNDRD_APPRCH_RSK_MSR_OTC_PSTNS
Functional NameFundamental review of the trading book standard approach risk measure for OTC positions
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA measured risk of an OTC position against a particular risk factor
Notes
Number Of Columns18
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1FNDMNTL_RVW_TRDNG_BK_STNDRD_APPRCH_RSK_MSR_IDPYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
5INSTRMNT_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
7RSK_FCTR_IDFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8CRRNCY_RCRDYString (3) DOMSubdomain for domain ISO-4217 Currency Codes (ISO4217 Domain) (Input Layer)
9RSK_MSR_TYPYString (1) DOMRisk measure type
10RSK_MSR_TYP_PSTNYString (1) DOMRisk measure type by position
11SHCK_DRCTNYString (1) DOMShock direction
12CRVTR_RSK_WGHTDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
13CRVTR_VLTN_DFFRNCDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
14DLT_SNSTVTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
15OPTN_MTRTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
16TNRDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
17UNDRLYNG_RSDL_MTRTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
18VG_SNSTVTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
2DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
3RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
4ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5INSTRMNT_IDAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
7RSK_FCTR_IDRisk factor identifier is a number that uniquely identifies the Risk factor.
8CRRNCY_RCRDCurrency of record is the currency in which the curvature risk is measured.
9RSK_MSR_TYPRisk measure type distinguishes Fundamental review of the trading book standard approach risk measure according to its nature.
10RSK_MSR_TYP_PSTNA discriminator to distinguish between different types of risk measures by position.
11SHCK_DRCTNThe direction of the change in uderlying (up or down), for each non-linear position we will have an upshift and a downshift measure recorded.
12CRVTR_RSK_WGHTThe weight associated with the curvature.
13CRVTR_VLTN_DFFRNCCurvature recorded as a difference between the normal valuation and  a revaluation after a small  increase OR decrease in an underyling risk factor, this is an amount interprted in the curency of record, e.g. 100 dollars. We only use carvature for non-linear instruments (e.g. options).
14DLT_SNSTVTYAn amount interpreted in the currency of the record, e.g., 100 dollars.
15OPTN_MTRTYThe option maturity to which this sensitivity relates.
16TNRThe tenor point of the related curve if this is a risk to a curve based risk factor.
17UNDRLYNG_RSDL_MTRTYWhere required, the underlying residual maturity to which the sensitivity is wwa created. Required when we consider that the sensitivity came from a change in a volatility surface.
18VG_SNSTVTYThe sensitivity, as an amount (e.g. 100 dollars).

Table NameFNNCL_CNTRCT
Functional NameFinancial contract
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Financial contract is a legally binding document under which one or multiple Instruments and / or Credit facilities are created.
Notes
Number Of Columns9
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1DT_RFRNCPYDateDOMAll possible dates
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4ACCNTNG_STNDRDPYString (1) DOMAccounting framework
5FNNCL_CNTRCT_IDPYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6SYNDCTD_CNTRCT_IDFString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7FNNCL_CNTRCT_INCPTN_DTYDateDOMAll possible dates
8FNNCL_CNTRCT_TYPYString (1) DOMFinancial contract type
9DT_LGL_FNL_MTRTYDateDOMAll possible dates
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5FNNCL_CNTRCT_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each financial contract.
6SYNDCTD_CNTRCT_ID 'Contract identifier' applied by the lead arranger of the syndicated contract to uniquely identify each contract. Each syndicated contract will have one 'syndicated contract identifier'. This value will not change over time and cannot be used by the lead arranger as the contract identifier for any other contract. All creditors participating in the syndicated contract must use the same 'syndicated contract identifier'.
7FNNCL_CNTRCT_INCPTN_DTThe date on which the current contractual relationship originated, i.e. the date on which the contract agreement became binding for all parties.
8FNNCL_CNTRCT_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between different types of Financial contracts.
9DT_LGL_FNL_MTRTYThe contractual maturity date of the asset or liability, taking into account any agreements amending initial contracts.
Indexes

Order

Table NameFNNCL_GRNT_INSTRMNT_DBT_SCRT_DBT_SCRTY_ASSGNMNT
Functional NameFinancial guarantee instrument covering a Debt security Debt security assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Financial guarantee instrument covering a Debt security Debt security assignment is the combination of a Financial guarantee covering a Debt security and a Debt security that indicates which Financial guarantee covering a Debt security is assigned to which Debt security.
Notes
Number Of Columns7
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1SCRTY_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
5ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
6INSTRMNT_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7PRTCTN_ALLCTD_VLYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1SCRTY_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
2DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
3RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
4ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6INSTRMNT_IDAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
7PRTCTN_ALLCTD_VLThe maximum amount of the protection value that can be considered as credit protection for the instrument. The amount of the existing third parties or observed agent priority claims against the protection must be excluded in the protection allocated value. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.

Table NameFR_VL_DCRS_CNTNGNT_ENCMBRNC
Functional Name30% Fair value decrease contingent encumbrance
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionContingent encumbrance refers to the additional assets which may need to be encumbered when the reporting institutions faces adverse developments triggered by an external event over which the reporting institution has no control (including a downgrade, decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets or a general loss of confidence). In these cases, the reporting institution will need to encumber additional assets as a consequence of already existing transactions. The additional amount of encumbered assets shall be net of the impact of the institution’s hedge transactions against the events described under the aforementioned stressed scenarios. (Annext 17 (Asset Encumbrance) 4. Part C)
Notes
Number Of Columns17
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1DT_RFRNCPYDateDOMAll possible dates
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level
4ACCNTNG_STNDRDPYString (1) DOMAccounting framework
5CLLTRLSD_DPSTS_OTHR_RPRCHS_AGRMNTS_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
6CLLTRLSD_DPSTS_OTHR_RPRCHS_AGRMNTS_CNTRL_BNKS_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
7DBT_SCRTY_ISSD_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
8DBT_SCRTY_ISSD_CVRD_BNDS_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
9DBT_SCRTY_ISSD_SCRTSTNS_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
10DPSTS_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
11DRVTVS_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
12OTC_DRVTV_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
13OTHR_SRCS_ENCMBRNC_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
14RPRCHS_AGRMNT_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
15RPRCHS_AGRMNT_CNTRL_BNK_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
16SLCTD_FNNCL_LBLT_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
17TTL_SRCS_ENCMBRNC_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5CLLTRLSD_DPSTS_OTHR_RPRCHS_AGRMNTS_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTCollateralised deposits other than repurchase agreements additional encumbered carrying amount in scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets that are Collateralised deposits other than repurchase agreements by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.
6CLLTRLSD_DPSTS_OTHR_RPRCHS_AGRMNTS_CNTRL_BNKS_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTCollateralised deposits other than repurchase agreements with central banks additional encumbered carrying amount in scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets that are Collateralised deposits other than repurchase agreements with central banks by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.
7DBT_SCRTY_ISSD_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTDebt securities issued additional encumbered carrying amount in scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets that are Debt securities issued by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.
8DBT_SCRTY_ISSD_CVRD_BNDS_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTDebt securities issued as covered bonds additional encumbered carrying amount in scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets that are Debt securities issued as covered bonds by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.
9DBT_SCRTY_ISSD_SCRTSTNS_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTDebt securities issued as securitisations additional encumbered carrying amount in scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets that are Debt securities issued as securitisations by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.
10DPSTS_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTDeposits additional encumbered carrying amount in scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets that are deposits by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.
11DRVTVS_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTDerivatives additional encumbered carrying amount in scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets that are derivatives by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.
12OTC_DRVTV_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTOver-The-Counter (OTC) derivatives additional encumbered carrying amount in scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets that are Over-The-Counter (OTC) derivatives by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.
13OTHR_SRCS_ENCMBRNC_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTOther sources of encumbrance additional encumbered carrying amount in scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets that are Other sources of encumbrance by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.
14RPRCHS_AGRMNT_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTRepurchase agreements additional encumbered carrying amount in  scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets that are repurchase agreements by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.
15RPRCHS_AGRMNT_CNTRL_BNK_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTRepurchase agreements with central banks additional encumbered carrying amount in  scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets that are Repurchase agreements with central banks by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.
16SLCTD_FNNCL_LBLT_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTSelected financial liabilities additional encumbered carrying amount in  scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the selected financial liabilities encumbered assets by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.
17TTL_SRCS_ENCMBRNC_ADDTNL_ENCMBRD_CRRYNG_AMNTTotal sources of encumbrance additional encumbered carrying amount in scenario A simulates the situation in which there is a decrease of the fair value of the encumbered assets by 30%. This fair value reduction could cause some of the liabilities that are the source of encumbrance to require for more protection and therefore for new assets to be encumbered. In template F 34.00.b the institution needs to report the amount of this additional encumbered assets in the eventuality of scenario A.

Table NameGRP_KY_MNGMNT_PRSNLL_ASSGNMNT
Functional NameGroup Key management personnel assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionGroup - Key management personnel assignment is the generalisation of the relationship types between any Group and any Key management personnel.
Notes
Number Of Columns8
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2NTRL_PRSN_GRP_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
3DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
4RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
6ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
7GRP_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8GRP_KY_MNGMNT_PRSNNL_ASSGNMNT_TYPYString (1) DOMGroup Key management personnel assignment type
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1PRTY_IDKey management personnel Party identifier is an identifier that uniquely identifies a party that acts in the Natural person group role as Key management personnel.
2NTRL_PRSN_GRP_RL_TYPNatural person group role type indicates the role that the natural person has for the group.
3DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
4RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
7GRP_IDInternal group identifier is an identifier to uniquely identify each internal group involved in the Internal group Key management personnel assignment.
8GRP_KY_MNGMNT_PRSNNL_ASSGNMNT_TYPGroup Key management personnel assignment type distinguishes the relationship type between Group and Key management personnel according to their nature.

Table NameIMMDT_PRNT_ENTRPRS_ASSGNMNT
Functional NameImmediate parent enterprise assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionImmediate parent assignment is the combination of a Central bank and private sector company and an Immediate parent that indicates which Central bank and private sector company has the role of immediate parent for which Central bank and private sector company.
Notes
Number Of Columns9
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1IMMDT_PRNT_CNTRL_BNK_PRVT_SCTR_CMPNY_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
5ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
6IMMDT_PRNT_ENTRPRS_ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
7IMMDT_PRNT_ENTRPRS_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8IMMDT_PRNT_RGLTN_867_2016_INDCTRYString (1) DOMImmediate parent according to AnaCredit indicator
9PRCNTG_OWNRSHPDECIMALDOMReal numbers from 0 to 1 with 6 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1IMMDT_PRNT_CNTRL_BNK_PRVT_SCTR_CMPNY_PRTY_IDThe party identifier of the involved central bank and private sector company.
2DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
3RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
4ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6IMMDT_PRNT_ENTRPRS_ENTTY_RL_TYPImmediate parent enterprise Party role type is the role type identifying the central bank and private sector company role type as Immediate parent enterprise.
7IMMDT_PRNT_ENTRPRS_PRTY_IDImmediate parent enterprise Party identifier identifies the party that has the Central bank and private sector company role type of Immediate parent enterprise in the Immediate parent enterprise assignment.
8IMMDT_PRNT_RGLTN_867_2016_INDCTRAn indicator specifying if the immediate parent is the immediate parent enterprise according to Regulation (EU) 867/2016, data attribute Immediate parent undertaking identifier; "Counterparty identifier for the legal entity which is the immediate parent undertaking of the counterparty. If the counterparty has no parent undertaking, the counterparty identifier for the counterparty itself is to be reported. Parent undertaking has the same meaning as defined in Article 4(1)(15)(a) of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.".
9PRCNTG_OWNRSHPThe percentage of ownership of the immediate parent as expressed in the Immediate parent assignment.

Table NameINSTRMNT
Functional NameFactoring cash reserve
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Instrument is a product that establishes a financial relationship between a bank and its customer.
In the context of accounting such a financial relationship may be considered a financial asset or a financial liabilty or an off-balance sheet item.
Notes
Number Of Columns34
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4INSTRMNT_IDPYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
6BLNC_SHT_NTTNG_IDFString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7CRDT_FCLTY_IDFString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8FNNCL_CNTRCT_IDFString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
9CRRNCYYString (3) DOMSubdomain for domain ISO-4217 Currency Codes (ISO4217 Domain) (Input Layer)
10EHQLA_INDCTRYString (1) DOM(Extremely) High liquidity and credit quality (HQLA) indicator (Input Layer)
11ELGBL_CNTRL_BNK_FNDNG_INDCTRYString (1) DOMEligible for central bank fundung (Input Layer)
12INSTRMNT_TYP_ORGNYString (1) DOMInstrument type by origin
13INSTRMNT_TYP_PRDCTYString (4) DOMType of instrument (Input layer)
14LTGTN_STTSYString (1) DOMLitigation status (Input Layer)
15OVRDRFT_INDCTRYString (1) DOMOverdraft indicator (Input layer)
16RPYMNT_RGHTSYString (1) DOMRepayment rights (Input Layer)
17RVCBL_INDCTRYString (1) DOMRevocable indicator (Input Layer)
18RVLVNG_LN_INDCTRYString (1) DOMRevolving loan indicator (Input Layer)
19SGNFCNT_ASST_CLSSYString (1) DOMSignificant asset class (Input Layer)
20SNDCTN_SB_PRTCPTN_MMBR_INSTRMNT_INDCTRYString (1) DOMSyndication or sub-participation member instrument indicator (Input layer)
21TYP_CMMTMNTYString (2) DOMType of commitment (Input Layer)
22TYP_RSKYString (1) DOMType of risk (Input Layer)
23CSH_RSRV_AMNTDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
24DT_CSH_RSRV_MTRTYDateDOMAll possible dates
25DT_INCPTNDateDOMAll possible dates
26DT_LGL_FNL_MTRTYDateDOMAll possible dates
27DT_ORGNL_CSH_RSRV_AMNTDateDOMAll possible dates
28DT_STTLMNTDateDOMAll possible dates
29MNMM_RSRV_AMNTDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
30NMNL_AMNTDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
31NTNL_AMNTDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
32OFF_BLNC_SHT_AMNTDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
33ORGNL_CSH_RSRV_AMNTDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
34UPIString (50) DOMString up to 50 characters
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
4INSTRMNT_IDAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
6BLNC_SHT_NTTNG_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each a balance sheet netting.
7CRDT_FCLTY_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each credit facility.
8FNNCL_CNTRCT_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each financial contract.
9CRRNCYThe currency code according to ISO4217.
10EHQLA_INDCTRAn indicator specifying if an asset or collateral received is considered extremely high liquidity and credit quality (HQLA) eligible according to Regulation (EU) 680/2014, Annex 17 (Asset encumbrance), part 2, 2.1.2, column 035.
11ELGBL_CNTRL_BNK_FNDNG_INDCTRSpecifies if an object is eligible for central bank funding in the sense that it might be exchanged / provided as collateral for funding.
12INSTRMNT_TYP_ORGNA discriminator to distinguish instrument by their type of origin. For example instruments arising from financial contracts or instruments arising from credit facilities.
14LTGTN_STTSLitigation indicates the status of the litigation that the exposure is in.

Finrep Annex V,  Part 2.321, 322: An exposure shall be ‘in pre-litigation status’ where the debtor has been formally notified that the institution will take legal action against the debtor within a defined time period, unless certain contractual or other payment obligations are met. That shall also include cases where the contract has been terminated by the reporting institution because the debtor is in formal breach of the terms and conditions of the contract and the debtor has been notified accordingly, but no legal action against the debtor has formally been taken by the institution yet. Exposures classified as ‘in pre-litigation status’ can exit this classification if the outstanding amounts are paid or if they enter into litigation status as defined in the following paragraph. 
An exposure shall be ‘in litigation status’ where legal action against the debtor has formally been taken. This comprises cases where a court of law confirmed that formal judiciary proceedings have occurred or the judiciary system has been notified of the intention to commence legal proceedings.
15OVRDRFT_INDCTROverdraft indicator indicates whether the Transferable deposit is in an overdraft situation or not. 

Overdraft is a debit balance on a current account. As defined in point 2(1)(c) of the Table in Part 2 of Annex II to Regulation (EU) No 2021/379 (BSI)
16RPYMNT_RGHTSRepayment rights, e.g. redeemable at notice.
17RVCBL_INDCTRRevocable indicator is an indicator specifying if the Other commitment is revocable or not
18RVLVNG_LN_INDCTRAn indicator specifying if an instrument is considered revolving or non-revolving.
19SGNFCNT_ASST_CLSSClassification into specific asset classes, like interest rate, equity, foreign exchange.
20SNDCTN_SB_PRTCPTN_MMBR_INSTRMNT_INDCTRSyndication or sub-participation member instrument indicator distinguishes Instrument between those that are a member of a syndicated loan or a sub-participation, and those that are not.
21TYP_CMMTMNTType of commitment.
22TYP_RSKType of financial risk Indicates the type of risk arising from exposures or transactions (e.g. credit risk or market risk) for Over the counter (OTC) Derivative instrument.
23CSH_RSRV_AMNTThe cash reserve of a factoring operation.
24DT_CSH_RSRV_MTRTY The contractual maturity date of the cash reserve (acting as protection), which is the earliest date at which the cash reserve may terminate or be terminated, taking into account any agreements amending initial contracts.
25DT_INCPTNThe date on which the current contractual relationship originated, i.e. the date on which the contract agreement became binding for all parties.
26DT_LGL_FNL_MTRTYThe contractual maturity date of the asset or liability, taking into account any agreements amending initial contracts.
27DT_ORGNL_CSH_RSRV_AMNTOriginal cash reserve amount date.
28DT_STTLMNTThe date on which the conditions specified in the contract are or can be executed for the first time, i.e. the date on which financial instruments are initially exchanged or created.
29MNMM_RSRV_AMNTMinimum reserve iamount is the amount, expressed in the currency of the Loan on demand, that is put into reserve at another instition and should be seen as encumbered. This is very often at a Central bank, but could also be at the overall group of savings banks, which in its turn puts it up as minimum reserve at their cental bank.

This amount is calculated by the bank itself based on their respective interpretation and choices of the accounting standard.
30NMNL_AMNTHolding amount at nominal value
31NTNL_AMNTThe notional amount on a financial instrument is the nominal or face amount that is used to calculate payments made on that instrument.
32OFF_BLNC_SHT_AMNTTotal nominal amount of off-balance sheet exposures. This includes any commitment to lend before considering conversion factors and credit risk mitigation techniques. It is the amount that best represents the institution's maximum exposure to credit risk without taking into account any protection held or other credit enhancements.
33ORGNL_CSH_RSRV_AMNTThe original cash reserve amount of a factoring operation.
34UPIThe UPI’s purpose is to uniquely identify any OTC derivative product that an authority requires to be reported to a Trade Repository

EHQLA_INDCTR
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1(Extremely) High liquidity and credit quality (HQLA)
2(Extremely) Not high liquidity and credit quality (HQLA)
ELGBL_CNTRL_BNK_FNDNG_INDCTR
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable: To be used if central bank eligibility does not apply.
1Eligible for central bank funding: Specifies if an object is eligible for central bank funding in the sense that it might be exchanged / provided as collateral for funding.
2Not elibible for central bank funding: Specifies if an object is not eligible for central bank funding.
INSTRMNT_TYP_ORGN
Values List
ValueDescription
3Instrument resulting directly from a Financial contract
4Instrument resulting from a Credit facility
INSTRMNT_TYP_PRDCT
Values List
ValueDescription
10OTC Option other than OTC Credit spread option
1003Reverse repurchase agreement instrument
1011Other commitment
1020Factoring
1022Other loan
1023Other trade receivables
120Transit items
1201Loan on demand used for minimum reserve
1202On demand deposit not used for minimum reserve
13Financial guarantee instrument for a Debt security
130Suspence items
14Financial guarantee instrument not for a Debt security
140Other advance
162Open repurchase agreement instrument
163Term repurchase agreement instrument
2Equity instrument that is not a security
26Security against a fee borrowing and lending transaction
27Security against Security borrowing and lending transaction
380Forward
5Other OTC Derivative instrument
51Credit card debt
511Tranferable deposit
512Other overnight deposits
522Deposits with agreed maturity - other than counterpart liability to non-derecognised loans
6OTC Total return swap
7OTC Credit default swap
8Other OTC Swap
80Finance leases
9OTC Credit spread option
LTGTN_STTS
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1In pre-litigation
2In litigation
3Not in litigation/pre-litigation
OVRDRFT_INDCTR
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Overdraft deposit
2Deposit not in overdraft
RPYMNT_RGHTS
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1On demand or short notice: Instruments which are repayable on demand or at short notice at the request of the creditor.
2Other than on demand or short notice: Instruments subject to repayment rights other than on demand or at short notice.
RVCBL_INDCTR
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Revocable
2Not revocable
RVLVNG_LN_INDCTR
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Revolving loan
2Not revolving loan
SGNFCNT_ASST_CLSS
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Interest rate
2Equity
3Credit
4Commodity
5Other
SNDCTN_SB_PRTCPTN_MMBR_INSTRMNT_INDCTR
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Syndication or sub-participation member instrument
2Non-syndicated contract nor sub-participation member instrument
TYP_CMMTMNT
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Forward deposits
10Loans commitments received
11Other commitments received
12To lend or to provide acceptance facilities at a below-market interest rate
13To lend or to provide acceptance facilities under pre-specified terms and conditions other than those at a below-market interest rate
14To lend or to provide acceptance facilities where the terms and conditions are not under pre-specified
15To provide guarantees
16To purchase securities
17For tender and performance guarantees
18Other Credit Facilities: Other
2Unpaid portion of partly-paid shares and securities
3Documentary credits issued or confirmed
4Trade finance off-balance sheet items
5Documentary credits in which underlying shipment acts as collateral and other self-liquidating transactions
6Warranties and indemnities’ (including tender and performance bonds) and ‘guarantees not having the character of credit substitutes
7Shipping guarantees, customs and tax bonds
8Note issuance facilities’ (NIFs) and ‘Revolving underwritings facilities’ (RUFs)
9Other off-balance sheet items
TYP_RSK
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not Applicable
1Interest rate risk: Type of risk in accordance with Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.129 (a)
2Equity risk: Type of risk in accordance with Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.129 (b)
3Foreign-exchange and gold risk: Type of risk in accordance with Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.129 (c)
4Credit risk: Type of risk in accordance with Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.129 (d)
5Commodities risk: Type of risk in accordance with Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.129 (e)
6Risk other than Interest rate risk, Equity risk, Foreign-exchange and gold risk, Credit risk, Commodities risk: Type of risk in accordance with Reg. 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.129 (f)

Table NameINSTRMNT_CLLTRL_ASSGNMNT
Functional NameInstrument Collateral assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Instrument Collateral assignment is the combination of an Instrument and a Collateral that indicates which Instrument is protected by which Collateral.
Notes
Number Of Columns11
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
4ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5INSTRMNT_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6CLLTRL_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7CLLTRL_RL_TYPPFYString (1) DOMCollateral role type (Input layer)
8INSTRMNT_CLLTRL_ASSGNMNT_TYPYString (1) DOMInstrument Collateral assignment type
9PRTCTN_ALLCTD_VLYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
10ALLCTD_UNSD_CLLTRL_VLDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
11THRD_PRTY_PRRTY_CLMSDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5INSTRMNT_IDAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
6CLLTRL_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each collateral.
7CLLTRL_RL_TYPCollateral role type distiguishes the Collateral role according to the role the Collateral acts in.
8INSTRMNT_CLLTRL_ASSGNMNT_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between different types of Instrument Collateral received instrument assignments.
9PRTCTN_ALLCTD_VLThe maximum amount of the protection value that can be considered as credit protection for the instrument. The amount of the existing third parties or observed agent priority claims against the protection must be excluded in the protection allocated value. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
10ALLCTD_UNSD_CLLTRL_VLAllocated unused collateral value is an amount that expresses the protection value that is not used for risk mitigation and, for FINREP reporting purposes is allocated to a specific Loan and advance.
11THRD_PRTY_PRRTY_CLMSThe maximum amount of any existing higher ranked liens with respect to third parties other than the observed agent against the protection.
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort
Order
INSTRMNT_CLLTRL_ASSGNMNT_PKv5PKDT_RFRNCASC
RPRTNG_AGNT_IDASC
ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLASC
ACCNTNG_STNDRDASC
INSTRMNT_IDASC
CLLTRL_IDASC
CLLTRL_RL_TYPASC
Constraints
TypeColumn / Constraint NameDetails
Column LevelACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVL
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1National GAAP not consistent with IFRS
2IFRS
3National GAAP consistent with IFRS
ACCNTNG_STNDRD
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1National GAAP not consistent with IFRS
2IFRS
3National GAAP consistent with IFRS
CLLTRL_RL_TYP
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Collateral received
2Collateral given
INSTRMNT_CLLTRL_ASSGNMNT_TYP
Values List
ValueDescription
1Loan and advance Collateral received assignment
2Off-balance instrument Collateral received assignment
4Off-balance instrument Collateral given assignment
5Deposit Collateral given assignment
6Over the counter (OTC) Derivative instrument Collateral given assignment
7(Reverse) repurchase transaction gold collateral received assignment
ALLCTD_UNSD_CLLTRL_VL
Ranges
Begin ValueEnd ValueDescription
0

Table NameINSTRMNT_CLLTRL_INSTRMNT_ASSGNMNT
Functional NameInstrument Collateral instrument assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Instrument Collateral  instrument assignment is the combination of an Instrument and a Collateral received instrument or Collateral given instrument that indicates which Instrument is protected by which Collateral received instrument or which instrument is being protected by which Collateral given instrument.
Notes
Number Of Columns9
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2INSTRMNT_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
5INSTRMNT_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMInstrument role type
6CLLTRL_INSTRMNT_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
8INSTRMNT_CLLTRL_RCVD_INSTRMNT_ASSGNMNT_TYPYString (1) DOMInstrument Collateral instrument assignment type
9PRTCTN_ALLCTD_VLYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
2INSTRMNT_IDAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
4DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
6CLLTRL_INSTRMNT_IDAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
8INSTRMNT_CLLTRL_RCVD_INSTRMNT_ASSGNMNT_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between different types of Instrument Collateral received instrument assignments.
9PRTCTN_ALLCTD_VLThe maximum amount of the protection value that can be considered as credit protection for the instrument. The amount of the existing third parties or observed agent priority claims against the protection must be excluded in the protection allocated value. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.

Table NameINSTRMNT_ENTTY_RL_ASSGNMNT
Functional NameInstrument Entity role assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Instrument Entity role assignment is the combination of an Instrument and an Entity role that indicates which Instrument is assigned to which Entity role.
Notes
Number Of Columns13
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
2INSTRMNT_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
5PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
7ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)22
8INSTRMNT_ENTTY_RL_ASSGNMNT_TYPYString (2) DOMInstrument Entity role assignment type
9LTGTN_STTSYString (1) DOMLitigation status (Input Layer)
10MN_DBTR_INDCTRYString (1) DOMMain debtor indicator (Input Layer)
11MN_DPST_DPST_TKNG_CRPRTN_INDCTRYString (1) DOMMain Deposit-taking corporation indicator (Input Layer)
12MN_LSS_INDCTRYString (1) DOMMain lessee indicator (Input Layer)
13JNT_LBLTY_AMNTDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
2INSTRMNT_IDAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
4DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
5PRTY_IDThe party identifier of the involved servicer.
7ENTTY_RL_TYPThe servicer role.
8INSTRMNT_ENTTY_RL_ASSGNMNT_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between different assignments between instruments and party roles, for example Deposit-taking corporation deposit assignment.
9LTGTN_STTSFinrep Annex V,  Part 2.321, 322: An exposure shall be ‘in pre-litigation status’ where the debtor has been formally notified that the institution will take legal action against the debtor within a defined time period, unless certain contractual or other payment obligations are met. That shall also include cases where the contract has been terminated by the reporting institution because the debtor is in formal breach of the terms and conditions of the contract and the debtor has been notified accordingly, but no legal action against the debtor has formally been taken by the institution yet. Exposures classified as ‘in pre-litigation status’ can exit this classification if the outstanding amounts are paid or if they enter into litigation status as defined in the following paragraph. 
An exposure shall be ‘in litigation status’ where legal action against the debtor has formally been taken. This comprises cases where a court of law confirmed that formal judiciary proceedings have occurred or the judiciary system has been notified of the intention to commence legal proceedings.
10MN_DBTR_INDCTRAn indicator specifying if a debtor is the main debtor.
11MN_DPST_DPST_TKNG_CRPRTN_INDCTRAn indicator specifying if a debtor is the main debtor.
12MN_LSS_INDCTRAn indicator specifying if a debtor is the main debtor.
13JNT_LBLTY_AMNTOutstanding nominal amount for which each debtor is liable in relation to a single instrument where there are two or more debtors.

Table NameINSTRMNT_HDGD_EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV
Functional NameInstrument hedged by Exchange tradable derivative position
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Instrument hedged by exchange tradable derivative position is the combination of an Instrument and an Exchange tradable derivative position as a hedge that indicates which Instrument is hedged by which Exchange tradable derivative position, acting in a the role of an Exchange tradable derivative position as a hedge.
Notes
Number Of Columns11
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_SCRTY_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6SLLR_ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
7SLLR_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8BYR_ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
9BYR_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
10INSTRMNT_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
11EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_PSTN_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMExchange tradable derivative position role type (Input Layer)
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5EXCHNG_TRDBL_DRVTV_SCRTY_IDThe security identifier of the exchange tradable derivative.
6SLLR_ENTTY_RL_TYPThe seller role.
7SLLR_PRTY_IDThe party identifier of the involved seller.
8BYR_ENTTY_RL_TYPThe buyer party role.
9BYR_PRTY_IDThe party identifier of the involved buyer.
10INSTRMNT_IDAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.

Table NameINSTRMNT_HDGD_OTC_DRVTV

Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionAn Instrument hedged by Over the counter (OTC) Derivative is the combination of an Instrument and an Over the counter (OTC) Derivative as a hedge that indicates which Instrument is hedged by which Over the counter (OTC) Derivative (acting in a the role of an Over the counter (OTC) Derivative as a hedge).

NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes

5OTC_DRVTV_IDAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.

NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
