62DRCGNSD_EXPSR_ACCMLTD_IMPRMNTDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
63DT_DFLT_STTSDateDOMAll possible dates
64DT_END_INTRST_ONLYDateDOMAll possible dates
65DT_FRBRNC_MSRSDateDOMAll possible dates
66DT_INCPTNDateDOMAll possible dates
67DT_NN_PRFRMNG_STTS_INSTRMNTDateDOMAll possible dates
68DT_NXT_INTRST_RT_RSTDateDOMAll possible dates
69DT_ORGNL_PRTCTN_VLDateDOMAll possible dates
70DT_PRTCTN_VLDateDOMAll possible dates
71DT_PST_DDateDOMAll possible dates
72DT_RNGTTN_STTSDateDOMAll possible dates
73DT_STTLMNTDateDOMAll possible dates
74FRBRNC_MSR_CNTIntegerDOMInteger
75FRZNG_PRD_DYSIntegerDOMNon-negative integers
76FVDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
77FV_CHNGDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
78FV_CHNG_CR_BFR_PRCHSDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
79FV_CHNGS_HDG_ACCNTNGDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
80GCA_EXPSR_DRCGNSD_EXCHNG_CLLTRLDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
81GNRL_ALLWNCS_BNK_RSKDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
82GNRL_ALLWNCS_CRDT_RSKDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
83GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNT_E_INTRSTDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
84GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNT_INTL_RCGNTNDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
85HRCTS_TRDNG_PSTN_FVDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
86INTRST_RT_CPDECIMAL (6)DOMReal numbers positive or negative with 6 decimals
87INTRST_RT_FLRDECIMAL (6)DOMReal numbers positive or negative with 6 decimals
88INTRST_RT_SPRDDECIMAL (6)DOMReal numbers positive or negative with 6 decimals
89ORGNL_PRTCTN_VLDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
90PRVSNS_OFF_BLNC_SHTDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
91STRT_DT_PRDDateDOMAll possible dates
92TKN_PSSSSN_DTDateDOMAll possible dates
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5INSTRMNT_IDAn identifier applied by the reporting agent to uniquely identify each instrument under a single contract. Each instrument must have one instrument identifier. This value will not change over time and cannot be used as the instrument identifier for any other instrument under the same contract.
7ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTNAccounting portfolio where the asset or liability is recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP –under Regulation (EU) 2015/534 (ECB/2015/13) applied by the observed agent's legal entity.
8ASST_ENCMBRNC_SRC_INDCTRAsset encumbrance source indicator signals whether the liabity or off-balance sheet item is a source of encumbrance for an asset or not.
9AVLBL_ENCMBRNC_INDCTRAvailable for encumbrance indicator distinguishes the asset into being available for asset encumbrance and not being available for asset encumbrance.
10BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_FNNCL_ASST_INSTRMNT_FR_VL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between different types of Balance sheet recognised financial asset instruments by fair value.
11BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_FNNCL_ASST_INSTRMNT_TKN_PSSSSN_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between Balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument obtained by taking possession and not obtained by taking into possesion
12CLLTRL_INSTRMNT_RS_INDCTRCollateral instrument reuse indicator distinguishes between Collateral received instruments that are reused as Collateral given instruments and those that are not reused.
13DFLT_STTSA discriminator to distinguish between default because unlikely to pay, 90/180 days past due or both according to Regulation (EU) 575/2013, Article 178.
14ELGBL_GRNT_INDCTREligible as guarantee indicator distinguishes the Collateral received instrument data into those where the Collateral received instrument, which is a Credit Default Swap, is eligible to be the a guarantee, or is not eligible to be a guarantee.
15ENCMBRD_ASST_INDCTREncumbered asset indicator signals whether the asset is encumbered or not.
16EXPSR_RCRS_INDCTRExposure with recourse indicator specifies if a Financial asset instrument is an instrument with or without recourse.
17FDCRYIdentification of instruments in which the observed agent acts in its own name but on behalf of and with the risk borne by a third party.
18FNNCL_ASST_INSTRMNT_TYP_CRR_123A discriminator to distinguish between financial assets that may potentially be classified as retail exposures and non-retail exposures.
19FNNCL_ASST_INSTRMNT_TYP_FXD_INTRST_RTA discriminator to distinguish between financial asset instruments with and without fixed interest rate.
20FNNCL_ASST_INSTRMNT_TYP_INTRST_RT_ONLA discriminator to distinguish between financial asset instruments with and without interest-only.
21FNNCL_ASST_INSTRMNT_TYP_RNGTTN_STTSA discriminator to distinguish between financial asset instruments with and without renegotiations.
22FRBRNC_MSR_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between refinanced debt, instruments with modified interest rate below market value and other modified terms.
23FV_HRRCHYLevel of fair value of a financial asset or liability according to the hierarchy defined in IFRS 13. Applicable to national GAAP based on BAD only if the allocation of instruments is measured at fair value according to different levels.
25HLD_SL_INDCTRIndicates the Non-current assets and instruments in disposal groups classified as held for sale in accordance with IFRS5. Is not applicable under national GAAP based on BAD.
26IMPRMNT_ASSSSMNT_MTHDThe method by which the impairment is assessed, if the instrument is subject to impairment in accordance with applied accounting standards. Collective and individual methods are distinguished.
27IMPRMNT_STTSType of impairment recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP.
28INTL_IMPRMNT_STTSInitial imapirment status is the impairment status, recorded in the beginning of the financial year or at the initial recognition of the impairment of the exposure after the beginning of the financial year.
29INTRST_RT_RST_FRQNCYInterest rate reset frequency is the frequency at which the interest rate is reset after the initial fixed-rate period, if any for the Financial asset instrument.
30LW_CRDT_RSK_INDCTRLow credit risk indicator for FINREP signals loan or advance has low credit risk or not, according to the Banks' internal assessments.
31MLTPL_FRBRNC_MSRS_INDCTRForbearance measures were granted in addition to already existing forbearance measures. It concerns forborne exposures under probation to which forbearance measures were applied in addition to forbearance measures granted at an earlier point in time, without the exposure having cured in between.
32NN_PRFRMNG_EXT_CRTR_MT_INDCTRNon-performing forborne exposures that failed to meet the non-performing exit criteria.
33NN_PRFRMNG_PRR_FRBRNC_INDCTRAn indicator specifying if an exposure was non-performing prior to the extension of forbearance measures.
34PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between trading and banking (or non-trading) book.
35PRFRMNG_FRBRN_EXPSR_UNDR_PRBTN_RCLSSFD_NN_PRFRMNG_INDCTRAn indicator specifying if an asset is classified as Non-performing forborne exposure under probation reclassified from non-performing or not according to Regulation (EU) 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.260.
36PRFRMNG_STTSPerforming status indicates how the off-balance sheet item given instrument performes in accordance with Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
37PRFRMNG_STTS_RSNThe reason for the choice of Performing status
38PRPSPurpose type is the classification of Financial asset instruments according to their purpose.
39PST_DU_FNNCL_ASST_INSTRMNT_INDCTRPast due financial instrument indicator distinguishes Financial instrument into whether or not its payments are being considered late, or past due.
40PYMNT_FRQNCYPayment frequency classifies the frequency of payments due, either of principal or interest, i.e. number of months between payments for the Financial asset instrument.
41RCGNTN_STTSBalance sheet recognition of the financial asset.
42RFRNC_RTReference rate used for the calculation of the actual interest rate.
43SBJCT_IMPRMNT_INDCTRSubject to impairment indicator indicates whether the Financial asset instrument is subject to impairment or not.
44SBRDNTD_DBTSubordinated debt indicator specifies if the Financial asset instrument is considered as subordinated debt or not.
45SPCLSD_LNDNG_EXPSR_TYPAn attribute  to specify what is the specialised lending type of an exposure.

According to CRR3 art 147 (8): Those exposures shall be assigned to the exposure class referred to in paragraph 2, point (c)(ii), and shall be categorised as follows: “project finance” (PF), “object finance” (OF), “commodity finance” (CF) and “income-producing real estate” (IPRE).’
46TYP_AMRTSTNAmortisation type of the Financial asset instrument including principal and interest.
47TYP_INTRST_RTType of interest rate classifies credit exposures based on the base rate for establishing the interest rate for each payment period.
48TYP_PRTCTN_VL_APPRCHType of protection valuation approach.
49ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FVAmount of gains and losses from re-measurement of the asset accumulated from the initial recognition to the reference date.
50ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FV_CRAccumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
51ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FV_EXPSR_CRAccumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014 of the exposure that is derecognised because of the asset obtained by taking possession.

Note: This amount must only be reported when it is negative.
52ACCMLTD_IMPRMNTThe amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the asset on the reporting reference date. This data attribute applies to assets subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.
53ACCMLTD_NGTV_VL_ADJSTMNT_CRAccumulated negative value adjustments. Market risk induced
54ACCMLTD_NGTV_VL_ADJSTMNT_MRAccumulated negative value adjustments. Market risk induced
55ACCMLTD_WRTFFSAccumulated write-offs is the Cumulative amount of principal and past due interest of any non-balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument that the institution is no longer recognising because they are considered uncollectible, independently of the portfolio in which they were included. Write-offs could be caused both by reductions in the carrying amount of financial assets recognised directly in profit or loss and by reductions in the amounts of the allowance accounts for credit losses set off against the carrying amount of financial assets.
56ACCRD_INTRSTThe amount of accrued interest on loans at the reporting reference date as defined in Regulation (EU) No 1071/2013 (ECB/2013/33). In accordance with the general principle of accruals accounting, interest receivable on instruments should be subject to on-balance sheet recording as it accrues (i.e. on an accruals basis) rather than when it is actually received (i.e. on a cash basis).
57ANNLSD_AGRD_RTInterest rate is the annualised agreed rate or narrowly defined interest rate in accordance with Regulation (EU) No 1072/2013 of the European Central Bank (ECB/2013/34) that is applicable to the Financial asset instrument.
58ARRRSArrears for the instrument is the aggregate amount of principal, interest and any fee payment outstanding at the reporting date, which is contractually due and has not been paid (past due) for the Financial asset instrument. This amount is always to be reported. '0' is to be reported if the instrument was not past due on the reporting date.
59CMLTV_RCVRS_SNC_DFLTThe total amount recovered since the date of default.
60CMMTMNT_INCPTNCommitment amount at inception is the Observed agent's maximum exposure to credit risk on the inception date of the financial asset instrument, without taking into account any protection held or other credit enhancements. Total commitment amount on the inception date is established during the approval process and is intended to restrict an observed agent's amount of credit risk to a given counterparty for the relevant instrument.
61CRRYNG_AMNTThe carrying amount in accordance with Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
62DRCGNSD_EXPSR_ACCMLTD_IMPRMNTAccumulated impairment is the amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the derecognised exposure on the reporting reference date for which the asset obtained by taking possession has been obtained.

This data attribute applies to assets subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.
63DT_DFLT_STTSThe date on which the default status is considered to have arisen.
64DT_END_INTRST_ONLYThe date on which the interest-only period ends. Interest-only is an instrument for which, for a contractually set period, only the interest on the principal balance is paid, with the principal balance remaining unchanged.
65DT_FRBRNC_MSRSThe date on which forbearance measure have been applied.
66DT_INCPTNFinancial asset instrument Inception date is the date on which the current contractual relationship originated, i.e. the date on which the contract agreement of the Financial asset instrument became binding for all parties.
67DT_NN_PRFRMNG_STTS_INSTRMNTThe date on which the non-performing status has been applied.
68DT_NXT_INTRST_RT_RSTNext interest rate reset date is the date that the next interest rate reset, as defined in Part 3 of Annex I to Regulation (EU) No 1071/2013 (ECB/2013/33), takes place for the Financial asset instrument. If the Financial asset instrument is not subject to a future interest rate reset, its legal final maturity date will be reported.
69DT_ORGNL_PRTCTN_VLThe date of the protection’s original value, i.e. the date on which the latest appraisal or valuation of the protection was carried out prior to its initial receipt as a credit protection.
70DT_PRTCTN_VLProtection value date is the date on which the latest appraisal or valuation of the protection was carried out prior to the reporting reference date.
71DT_PST_DDate past due is the date on which the Financial asset instrument became past due in accordance with Part 2.48 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014. This is the latest such date prior to the reporting reference date and it is to be reported if the instrument is past due on the reporting reference date.
72DT_RNGTTN_STTSThe date on which the renegotiation status is considered to have been established or changed.
73DT_STTLMNTSettlement date is the date on which the conditions specified in the contract are or can be executed for the first time, i.e. the date on which the financial asset instrument is initially exchanged or created.
74FRBRNC_MSR_CNTNumber of times an exposure has been under forbearance measures. Exposures classified as forborne at the reporting reference date, to which forbearance measures have been applied at two, respectively more than two different points in time. That includes, among others, originally forborne exposures that exited the forborne status (cured forborne exposures), but were granted new forbearance measures after that.
75FRZNG_PRD_DYSFreezing period in days is the number of days (if any) for which the freezing period of the Past due financial asset instrument has been applied, in accordance with EBA guidelines 2016/07 paragraph 18.
76FVFair value as defined in IFRS 13.9.
77FV_CHNGFair value changes in the period and before taxes.
78FV_CHNG_CR_BFR_PRCHSFair value changes due to changes in credit risk before purchase is the difference between the outstanding nominal amount and the purchase price of the Financial asset instrument at the purchase date.
79FV_CHNGS_HDG_ACCNTNGChanges in the fair value of an asset or liability, which is a hedged item and measured at amortised cost, that are recognised in the carrying amount due to the application of hedge accounting (IFRS 9.6)
81GNRL_ALLWNCS_BNK_RSKGeneral allowances for bank risk affecting carrying amount (nGAAP)
82GNRL_ALLWNCS_CRDT_RSKGeneral allowances for credit risk affecting carrying amount.
83GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNT_E_INTRSTGross carrying amount, as defined in IFRS 9 appendix A, excluding accrued interest
84GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNT_INTL_RCGNTNGross carrying amount at the point in time of the initial recognition in the balance sheet 
85HRCTS_TRDNG_PSTN_FVHaircut for trading position valued at fair value for the Balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument according to national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP). 
According to Annex V (5.29):
Where applicable under national GAAP based on BAD, ‘Haircuts for trading positions valued at fair value’ shall be reported. The haircuts decrease the value of trading assets and increase the value of trading liabilities.
86INTRST_RT_CPMaximum value for the interest rate charged.
87INTRST_RT_FLRMinimum value for the interest rate charged.
88INTRST_RT_SPRDMargin or spread (expressed as a percentage) to add to the reference rate that is used for the calculation of the interest rate in basis points.
89ORGNL_PRTCTN_VLThe protection’s fair value at the date when it was originally received as a credit protection.
90PRVSNS_OFF_BLNC_SHTThe amount of provisions for off-balance sheet amounts.
91STRT_DT_PRDThe start date of the period.
92TKN_PSSSSN_DTBalance sheet recognition date is a date on the asset obtained by taking possession  was taken into possession and thus recognised on the balance sheet.

ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTN
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
10 nGAAP: Loans and receivables
12nGAAP: Held-to-maturity investments
14IFRS: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with IFRS.
2IFRS: Financial assets held for trading: Financial assets held for trading in accordance with IFRS.
21IFRS: Financial liabilities measured at amortised cost: Financial liabilities measured at amortised cost in accordance with IFRS
23IFRS: Financial liabilities held for trading: Financial liabilities held for trading in accordance with IFRS
25IFRS: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with IFRS
3nGAAP: Trading Financial assets: Trading financial assets in accordance with national GAAP.
31nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial liabilities measured at a cost-based method: Non-trading non-derivative financial liabilities measured at a cost-based method accordance with national GAAP based on BAD
33nGAAP: Trading financial liabilities: Trading financial liabilities in accordance with national GAAP based on BAD
35nGAAP: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial liabilities designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with nationa GAAP based on BAD
4IFRS: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets measured at fair value through profit and loss and designated as such upon initial recognition or subsequently in accordance with IFRS, except those classified as financial assets held for trading.
41IFRS: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with IFRS.
45nGAAP: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with national GAAP.
46nGAAP: Financial assets held for trading
47nGAAP: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with national GAAP.
48nGAAP: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss
5nGAAP: Available-for-sale financial assets
6IFRS: Financial assets at amortised cost: Financial assets measured at amortised cost in accordance with IFRS.
64nGAAP: financial assets at fair value or strict LOCOM
7nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.
711Accounting portfolios for financial assets other than classified as held for sale, excluding financial assets held for trading, trading financial assets and cash and cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits
73nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. LOCOM: nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets at LOCOM
74nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. Other than LOCOM
76nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. LOCOM: nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method at LOCOM
77nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. Other than LOCOM
8IFRS: Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income: Financial assets measured at fair value through other comprehensive income due to business model and cash-flows characteristics in accordance with IFRS.
83Investments in subsidiaries, joint ventures and associates.
851nGAAP: Accounting portfolios for trading financial assetss. Cost based method or LOCOM
852nGAAP: Accounting portfolios for trading financial liabilities. Cost based method or LOCOM
9nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.
90Under IFRS 9 impairment: Off balance sheet accounting classification: under IFRS 9 impairment
911Measured under IAS 37: Off balance sheet accounting classification: measured under IAS 37
912Measured under IFRS 4: Off balance sheet accounting classification: measured under IFRS 4
92Measured at fair value through profit or loss: Off balance sheet accounting classification: IFRS 9 fair valued commitments and financial guarantees
93Under nGAAP: Off balance sheet accounting classification: measured under nGAAP based on BAD

Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
24Performing non-retail exposure class financial asset instrument
25Non-performing non-retail exposure class financial asset instrument
28Default financial asset instrument individually assessed
30Performing financial asset instrument debtor assessed
31Non-performing financial asset instrument debtor assessed
32Performing non-default financial asset instrument individually assessed
33Non-performing non-default financial asset instrument individually assessed
FNNCL_ASST_INSTRMNT_TYP_FXD_INTRST_RT
Values List

0Not applicable
18Fixed interest financial asset instrument
19Non-fixed interest financial asset instrument

15Renegotiated financial asset instrument with forbearance measure

FRBRNC_MSR_TYP
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Grace period/payment moratorium
10Forborne: instruments with modified interest rate below market conditions
11Forborne: instruments with modified interest rate not below market conditions
3Extension of maturity/term
4Rescheduled payments
5Debt forgiveness
6Debt asset swaps
7Other forbearance measures
8Forborne: Refinanced debt
9Forborne: instruments with other modified terms and conditions
FV_HRRCHY
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Level 1: Level 1 inputs used for the measurement of fair value. Level 1 inputs are quoted prices in active markets for identical assets or liabilities that the entity can access at the measurement date
2Level 2: Level 2 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 2 inputs are inputs other than quoted prices included within Level 1 that are observable for the asset or liability, either directly or indirectly
3Level 3: Level 3 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 3 inputs are unobservable inputs for the asset or liability
FVO_DSGNTN
Values List
ValueDescription
1Accounting mismatch
2Management on a fair value basis
5Management of credit risk. Upon designation
6Management of credit risk. After the designation
HLD_SL_INDCTR
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Held for sale: The member indicates that the instrument is held for sale
2Not held for sale: The member indicates that the instrument is not held for sale
IMPRMNT_ASSSSMNT_MTHD
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Collectively assessed
2Individually assessed
IMPRMNT_STTS
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
211General allowances for credit risk (GAAP)
212General allowances for banking risk (GAAP)
23Stage 1 (IFRS): To be used if the instrument is not impaired and a loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected credit losses is raised against the instrument under IFRS. Only for instruments subject to impairment under IFRS 9.
24Stage 2 (IFRS): To be used if the instrument is not impaired and a loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected credit losses is raised against the instrument under IFRS. Only for instruments subject to impairment under IFRS 9.
25Stage 3 (IFRS): To be used if the instrument is impaired and a loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected credit losses is raised against the instrument under IFRS. Only for instruments subject to impairment under IFRS 9.
26Specific allowances (GAAP): To be used if the instrument is subject to impairment in accordance with an applied accounting standard other than IFRS 9 and specific loss allowances are raised, irrespective of whether these allowances are individually or collectively assessed (impaired
27Purchased or originated credit-impaired instruments (POCI) (IFRS)
INTRST_RT_RST_FRQNCY
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Annual
12Other than overnight, monthly, quarterly, half yearly, annually or at creditor discretion
16Overnight
18Quarterly
19Semi-annually
3At creditor discretion
8Monthly

Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Low credit risk instrument
2Non-low credit risk instrument

ValueDescription

2Not multiple forbearance measures in place

ValueDescription

2Still able to meet the non-performing exit criteria

ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Non-performing prior to forbearance
2Not non-performing prior to forbearance

1Trading book
2Non-trading book

1Performing forborne exposure under probation
2Performing forborne exposure not under probation
PRFRMNG_STTS
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Non-performing
11Performing
PRFRMNG_STTS_RSN
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Failed reclassification to performing at end of probation period
2Exited from Non-performing exposure (NPE) in the last 12 months
PRPS
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Credit for consumption: Credit for consumption (a loan granted predominantly for the purpose of personal consumption of goods and services), in accordance with Part 2 of Annex II of Regulation (EU) No 1071/2013 (ECB/2013/33).
12Residential real estate purchase: Financing of residential property. Residential property is defined in Article 4(1)(75) of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013.
13Commercial real estate purchase: Financing of real estate property other than residential property.
19Other purposes: Purposes other than consumption, debt financing, residential and commercial real estate purchase, margin lending, imports, exports, construction investment and working capital facility.
4Margin lending: Instruments in which an institution extends credit in connection with the purchase, sale, carrying or trading of securities. Margin lending instruments do not include other loans that are secured by collateral in the form of securities.
5Debt financing: Financing of outstanding or maturing debt. This includes debt refinancing.
6Imports: Financing of goods and services (purchases, barter and/or gifts) from non-residents to residents.
7Exports: Financing of goods and services (sales, barter and/or gifts) from residents to non-residents.
8Construction investment: Financing of construction of buildings, infrastructure and industrial facilities.
9Working capital facility: Financing the cash flow management of an organisation.
PST_DU_FNNCL_ASST_INSTRMNT_INDCTR
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
48Past due financial asset instrument
49Not past due financial asset instrument
PYMNT_FRQNCY
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Annual
15Other than monthly, quarterly, half yearly, annually, bullet or zero coupon
18Quarterly
19Semi-annually
22Zero-coupon: Amortisation in which the full principal amount and interest is repaid in the last instalment.
4Bullet: Amortisation in which the full principal amount is repaid in the last instalment regardless of the interest payment frequency.
8Monthly
RCGNTN_STTS
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Entirely recognised: Instrument entirely recognised, in accordance with Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
2Recognised to the extent of the institution's continuing involvement
4Partially derecognised: Instrument partially derecognised

SBJCT_IMPRMNT_INDCTR
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Subject to impairment
2Not subject to impairment
SBRDNTD_DBT
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Subordinated debt
2Not subordinated debt
SPCLSD_LNDNG_EXPSR_TYP
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Project finance (PF)
2Object finance (OF)
3Commodity finance (CF)
4Income-producing real estate (IPRE)
5Not specialised lending exposure
TYP_AMRTSTN
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1French: Amortisation in which the total amount - principal plus interest - repaid in each instalment is the same.
2German: Amortisation in which the first instalment is interest-only and the remaining instalments are constant, including capital amortisation and interest.
3Fixed amortisation schedule: Amortisation in which the principal amount repaid in each instalment is the same.
4Bullet: Amortisation in which the full principal amount is repaid in the last instalment.
5Amortisation types other than French, German, Fixed amortisation schedule or bullet: Other amortisation schedule applied than the ones specified.
TYP_INTRST_RT
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Fixed: Scheme defining the interest rates during the life of the exposure which only includes constant rates - numeric constant rate known with certainty at the inception of the exposure - and where the interest rates apply to the whole exposure. The scheme may contain more than one constant interest rate to be applied at different periods during the life of the exposure (e.g. loan with a constant interest rate during the initial fixed-rate period, which then changes to a different interest rate, which is still constant, and which was known at the inception of the exposure).
2Variable: Scheme defining the interest rates during the life of the exposure which only includes interest rates based on the evolution of another variable (the reference variable) and where the interest rate applies to the whole exposure.
3Mixed: Interest rate class other than fixed or variable.
TYP_PRTCTN_VL_APPRCH
Values List
ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Counterparty estimation: Valuation method whereby the valuation is carried out by the provider of the protection.
2Creditor valuation: Valuation method whereby the valuation is carried out by the creditor: valuation undertaken by an external or staff appraiser who possesses the necessary qualifications, ability and experience to execute a valuation and who is not independent from the credit decision process.
3Mark-to-market: Valuation method whereby the protection value is based on unadjusted quoted prices for identical assets and liabilities in an active market.
4Other type of valuation: Other type of valuation not included in any other valuation categories.
5Third-party valuation: Valuation method in which the valuation is provided by an appraiser who is independent from the credit decision process.

Table NameINTRNL_GRP_RL
Functional NameInternal group role
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Internal group role is a role an Internal group may act in.
Notes
Number Of Columns11
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1GRP_RL_TYPPYString (1) DOMGroup role type
2DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
3RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
4GRP_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
6ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
7ACCNTNG_STNDRD_CNLDTD_RPRTNGYString (1) DOMAccounting framework
8ACCNTNG_STNDRD_SOLO_RPRTNGYString (1) DOMAccounting framework
9ACCNTNG_STNDRD_STTSTCL_RPRTNGYString (1) DOMAccounting framework
10AE_RPRTNG_SCHMYStringDOMAsset encumbrance reporting schemev1
11FNRP_RPRTNG_SCHMYStringDOMFinrep reporting scheme
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1GRP_RL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a group may act in.
2DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
3RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
4GRP_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each group.
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
7ACCNTNG_STNDRD_CNLDTD_RPRTNGAccounting standard for consolidated reporting is the accounting standard used by the Reporting agent internal group role for cons reporting.
8ACCNTNG_STNDRD_SOLO_RPRTNGAccounting standard for solo reporting is the accounting standard used by the Reporting agent internal group role for solo reporting.
9ACCNTNG_STNDRD_STTSTCL_RPRTNGAccounting standard for statistical reporting is the accounting standard used by the Reporting agent internal group role to report statistical data.

Table NameINTRST_RT_RSK_HDG_PRTFL
Functional NameInterest rate risk hedge portfolio
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionInterest rate risk hedge portfolio are the portfolio of instruments whose hedged postions need to be reported as one with the rows called 'Fair value changes of the hedged items in portfolio hedge of interest rate risk'.

See https://www.ifrs.org/content/dam/ifrs/meetings/2010/september/iasb/vbfi0910b14bobs.pdf
Notes
Number Of Columns7
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1DT_RFRNCPYDateDOMAll possible dates
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level
4ACCNTNG_STNDRDPYString (1) DOMAccounting framework
5DMSTC_ACTVT_INDCTRPYString (1) DOMDomestic activities indicator
6HDGD_PRTFL_ASST_LBLTY_INDCTRPYString (1) DOMHedged portfolio asset or liability indicator
7HDGD_ITMS_FV_CHNGSYDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5DMSTC_ACTVT_INDCTRDomestic activities indicator distinguishes the Interest rate risk hedge portfolio between domestic and non-domestic activities from the perspective of the reporting agent.
6HDGD_PRTFL_ASST_LBLTY_INDCTRHedged portfolio  asset or liability indicator classifies  the Interest rate risk hedge portfolio  into assets or liabilities.
7HDGD_ITMS_FV_CHNGSFair value changes of the hedged items in portfolio hedge of interest rate risk

Table NameINVSTMNT_PRPRTY_TKN_PSSSSN
Functional NameInvestment property taken into possession 
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionInvestment property taken into possession is an investment property that was pledged by the debtor as collateral, but was obtained in exchange for the cancellation of debt, whether on a voluntary basis or as part of legal proceedings.
Notes
Number Of Columns18
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
4ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
5HLD_SL_INDCTRPFYString (1) DOMHeld for sale indicator (Input Layer)
6MSRMNT_MTHDPYString (1) DOMMeasurement method
7SBJCT_OPRTNG_LS_INDCTRPYString (1) DOMSubject to operating lease indicator
8INVSTMNT_PRPRTY_TKN_PSSSSN_TYPPYString (2) DOMInvestment property type
9TKN_PSSSSN_DTPYDateDOMAll possible dates
10NN_FNNCL_ASST_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
11ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FV_EXPSR_CRYDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
12DRCGNSD_EXPSR_ACCMLTD_IMPRMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
13GCA_EXPSR_DRCGNSD_EXCHNG_CLLTRLYDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
14GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
15GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNT_INTL_RCGNTNYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
16NMBR_INVSTMNT_PRPRTY_TKN_PSSSSNYREALDOMNon-negative real numbers with 0 decimals
17PLNNNG_PRMSSN_INDCTRYString (1) DOMPlanning permission indicator (Input Layer)
18UNDR_CNSTRCTN_INDCTRYString (1) DOMUnder construction or development indicator (Input Layer)
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5HLD_SL_INDCTRHeld for sale  indicates the Non-current assets and instruments in disposal groups classified as held for sale in accordance with IFRS5. Is not applicable under national GAAP based on BAD.
6MSRMNT_MTHDMeasurement method identifies the type of measurement method according to IAS (See IAS 16.30, 16.31 and IAS 40.33-55, 76). It distinguishes into their cost or revaluation model. 
7SBJCT_OPRTNG_LS_INDCTRAn indicator to specify if an object is subject to operating lease or not.
9TKN_PSSSSN_DTBalance sheet recognition date is a date on the asset obtained by taking possession  was taken into possession and thus recognised on the balance sheet.
11ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FV_EXPSR_CRAccumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014 of the exposure that is derecognised because of the asset obtained by taking possession.

Note: This amount must only be reported when it is negative.
12DRCGNSD_EXPSR_ACCMLTD_IMPRMNTAccumulated impairment is the amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the derecognised exposure on the reporting reference date for which the asset obtained by taking possession has been obtained.

This data attribute applies to assets subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.
14GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNTGross carrying amount at the point in time of the initial recognition in the balance sheet 
15GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNT_INTL_RCGNTNGross carrying amount at the point in time of the initial recognition in the balance sheet 
17PLNNNG_PRMSSN_INDCTRPlanning permission for development indicator distinguishes Land excluding agriculture based on whether planning permission for development applies or not.
18UNDR_CNSTRCTN_INDCTRUnder construction or development indicator indicates whether the property is under construction or development.

Finrep Annex V. Part 2.350, 352(a). With regard to collateral in the form of immovable property (a) immovable property that is under construction or development.

5CLLTRL_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
6CLLTRL_RL_TYPPFYString (1) DOMCollateral role type (Input layer)
7INVSTR_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
8INVSTR_ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
9SCRTY_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
10SCRTY_PSTN_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_TYPPFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
11LNG_SHRT_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_TYPPFYStringDOMLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position type (Input Layer)
12ALLCTD_UNSD_CLLTRL_VLYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
13PRTCTN_ALLCTD_VLYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
2RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
3ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
5CLLTRL_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each collateral.
6CLLTRL_RL_TYPCollateral role type distiguishes the Collateral role according to the role the Collateral acts in.
7INVSTR_PRTY_IDAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
8INVSTR_ENTTY_RL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
9SCRTY_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
12ALLCTD_UNSD_CLLTRL_VLAllocated unused collateral value is an amount that expresses the protection value that is not used for risk mitigation and, for FINREP reporting purposes is allocated to a specific Loan and advance, which is the Long non-negotiable security position.
13PRTCTN_ALLCTD_VLProtection allocated value is the maximum amount of the protection value, that can be considered as credit protection for the Long non-negotiable security position. For protections that is eligible under Regulation (EU) No 575/2013, this value should be reported in accordance with Part 2 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.

Table NameLNG_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_PRDNTL_PRTFL_ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTN_ASSGNMNT
Functional NameLong balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Long balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment is the combination between Long balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio assignment and Accounting classifcation.
Notes
Number Of Columns44
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTNPYString (3) DOMAccounting classification for assets
2PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYPPFYString (1) DOMPrudential portfolio
3LNG_SHRT_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_TYPPFYStringDOMLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position type (Input Layer)

29LNG_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_PRDNTL_PRTFL_ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTN_ASSGNMNT_ACCNTNG_STNDRD_TYPYStringDOMLong balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment accounting standard type
30LNG_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_PRDNTL_PRTFL_ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTN_ASSGNMNT_TYPYStringDOMLong balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classifcation assignment type

Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTNAccounting portfolio where the asset or liability is recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP –under Regulation (EU) 2015/534 (ECB/2015/13) applied by the observed agent's legal entity.
2PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between trading and banking (or non-trading) book.
4RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6INVSTR_PRTY_IDAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
7INVSTR_ENTTY_RL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
8DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
10SCRTY_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
12ACCMLTD_IMPRMNTThe amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the asset on the reporting reference date. This data attribute applies to assets subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.
13ACCMLTD_NGTV_VL_ADJSTMNT_CRAccumulated negative value adjustments. Market risk induced
14ACCMLTD_NGTV_VL_ADJSTMNT_MRAccumulated negative value adjustments. Market risk induced
15ACCMLTD_WRTFFSCumulative amount of principal and past due interest of any debt instrument that the institution is no longer recognising because they are considered uncollectible, independently of the portfolio in which they were included. Write-offs could be caused both by reductions in the carrying amount of financial assets recognised directly in profit or loss and by reductions in the amounts of the allowance accounts for credit losses set off against the carrying amount of financial assets.
16ARRRSAggregate amount of principal, interest and any fee payment outstanding at the reporting date, which is contractually due and has not been paid (past due). This amount is always to be reported. '0' is to be reported if the instrument was not past due on the reporting date.
17AVLBL_ENCMBRNC_INDCTRAvailable for encumbrance indicator distinguishes the asset into being available for asset encumbrance and not being available for asset encumbrance.
18CRRYNG_AMNTThe carrying amount in accordance with Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
19ENCMBRD_ASST_INDCTREncumbered asset indicator signals whether the asset is encumbered or not.
20FVFair value as defined in IFRS 13.9.
21FV_CHNGFair value changes in the period and before taxes.
22FV_CHNGS_HDG_ACCNTNGChanges in the fair value of an asset or liability, which is a hedged item and measured at amortised cost, that are recognised in the carrying amount due to the application of hedge accounting (IFRS 9.6)
23FV_HRRCHYLevel of fair value of a financial asset or liability according to the hierarchy defined in IFRS 13. Applicable to national GAAP based on BAD only if the allocation of instruments is measured at fair value according to different levels.
25GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNT_E_INTRSTGross carrying amount, as defined in IFRS 9 appendix A, excluding accrued interest
26IMPRMNT_ASSMNT_MTHDThe method by which the impairment is assessed, if the instrument is subject to impairment in accordance with applied accounting standards. Collective and individual methods are distinguished.
27IMPRMNT_STTSType of impairment recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP.
28INTL_IMPRMNT_STTSInitial imapirment status is the impairment status, recorded in the beginning of the financial year or at the initial recognition of the impairment of the exposure after the beginning of the financial year.
29LNG_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_PRDNTL_PRTFL_ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTN_ASSGNMNT_ACCNTNG_STNDRD_TYPLong balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment accounting standard type classifies a Long balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment by accounting standard (nGAAP, IFRS)
30LNG_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_PRDNTL_PRTFL_ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTN_ASSGNMNT_TYPLong balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classifcation assignment type defines whether Long balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classifcation assignment is a debt secuirtyo r equity or fund.
31LTGTN_STTSFinrep Annex V,  Part 2.321, 322: An exposure shall be ‘in pre-litigation status’ where the debtor has been formally notified that the institution will take legal action against the debtor within a defined time period, unless certain contractual or other payment obligations are met. That shall also include cases where the contract has been terminated by the reporting institution because the debtor is in formal breach of the terms and conditions of the contract and the debtor has been notified accordingly, but no legal action against the debtor has formally been taken by the institution yet. Exposures classified as ‘in pre-litigation status’ can exit this classification if the outstanding amounts are paid or if they enter into litigation status as defined in the following paragraph. 
An exposure shall be ‘in litigation status’ where legal action against the debtor has formally been taken. This comprises cases where a court of law confirmed that formal judiciary proceedings have occurred or the judiciary system has been notified of the intention to commence legal proceedings.
32NN_PRFRMNG_PRR_FRBRNC_INDCTRNon-performing prior to forbearance specifies if an asset is classified as non-performing prior to forbearance according to Regulation (EU) 680/2014, Annex V, part 2.263.
33RTND_DBT_SCRTY_INDCTRRetained security indicator distinguishes the Long debt security position derived data into those positions that have been retained by the reporting agent and those that are not retained. 

The Long security debt position is retained if the investor is the reporting agent.
34SBJCT_IMPRMNT_INDCTRSubject to impairment indicator indicates whether the asset is subject to impairment or not.
35ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FVAmount of gains and losses from re-measurement of the asset accumulated from the initial recognition to the reference date.
36ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FV_CRAccumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014.
37ACCRD_INTRSTThe amount of accrued interest on loans at the reporting reference date as defined in Regulation (EU) No 1071/2013 (ECB/2013/33). In accordance with the general principle of accruals accounting, interest receivable on instruments should be subject to on-balance sheet recording as it accrues (i.e. on an accruals basis) rather than when it is actually received (i.e. on a cash basis).
38ENCMBRD_NMBR_SHRSThe number of shares that are encumbered according to ITS on Asset encumbrance, 1.7 Definition of encumbrance.
39GNRL_ALLWNCS_BNK_RSKGeneral allowances for bank risk affecting carrying amount (nGAAP)
40GNRL_ALLWNCS_CRDT_RSKGeneral allowances for credit risk affecting carrying amount.
41HRCTS_TRDNG_PSTN_FVHaircut for trading position valued at fair value. 
According to Annex V (5.29):
Where applicable under national GAAP based on BAD, ‘Haircuts for trading positions valued at fair value’ shall be reported. The haircuts decrease the value of trading assets and increase the value of trading liabilities.
42NMBR_SHRSThe number of shares of a security.
43NMNL_AMNTHolding amount at nominal value
44PRCNTG_RTNDPercentage retained is a percentage that states how much of the Long debt security positions nominal value is part of the Debt security issued (by the reporting agent) nominal value.
Indexes

Constraints
TypeColumn / Constraint NameDetails
Column LevelACCNTNG_CLSSFCTN
Values List
ValueDescription
10 nGAAP: Loans and receivables
12nGAAP: Held-to-maturity investments
14IFRS: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with IFRS.
2IFRS: Financial assets held for trading: Financial assets held for trading in accordance with IFRS.
3nGAAP: Trading Financial assets: Trading financial assets in accordance with national GAAP.
4IFRS: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets measured at fair value through profit and loss and designated as such upon initial recognition or subsequently in accordance with IFRS, except those classified as financial assets held for trading.
41IFRS: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with IFRS.
45nGAAP: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with national GAAP.
46nGAAP: Financial assets held for trading
47nGAAP: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with national GAAP.
48nGAAP: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss
5nGAAP: Available-for-sale financial assets
6IFRS: Financial assets at amortised cost: Financial assets measured at amortised cost in accordance with IFRS.
64nGAAP: financial assets at fair value or strict LOCOM
7nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.
711Accounting portfolios for financial assets other than classified as held for sale, excluding financial assets held for trading, trading financial assets and cash and cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits
73nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. LOCOM: nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets at LOCOM
74nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. Other than LOCOM
76nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. LOCOM: nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method at LOCOM
77nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. Other than LOCOM
8IFRS: Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income: Financial assets measured at fair value through other comprehensive income due to business model and cash-flows characteristics in accordance with IFRS.
83Investments in subsidiaries, joint ventures and associates.
851nGAAP: Accounting portfolios for trading financial assetss. Cost based method or LOCOM
9nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.

2Non-trading book

Values List

0Not applicable
1National GAAP not consistent with IFRS
2IFRS

SCRTY_PSTN_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_TYP
Values List
ValueDescription
1Balance sheet recognised security position
3Non-balance sheet recognised debt security position
4Non-balance sheet recognised equity or fund security position
AVLBL_ENCMBRNC_INDCTR
Values List
ValueDescription
1Available for encumbrance
2Not-available for encumbrance
ENCMBRD_ASST_INDCTR
Values List
ValueDescription
1Not encumbered asset
100Encumbered asset
FV_HRRCHY
Values List
ValueDescription
1Level 1: Level 1 inputs used for the measurement of fair value. Level 1 inputs are quoted prices in active markets for identical assets or liabilities that the entity can access at the measurement date
2Level 2: Level 2 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 2 inputs are inputs other than quoted prices included within Level 1 that are observable for the asset or liability, either directly or indirectly
3Level 3: Level 3 inputs used for the measurment of fair value. Level 3 inputs are unobservable inputs for the asset or liability
FVO_DSGNTN
Values List
ValueDescription
1Accounting mismatch
2Management on a fair value basis
3Management of credit risk. Upon designation
4Management of credit risk. After the designation
IMPRMNT_ASSMNT_MTHD
Values List
ValueDescription
1Collectively assessed
2Individually assessed
IMPRMNT_STTS
Values List
ValueDescription
211General allowances for credit risk (GAAP)
212General allowances for banking risk (GAAP)
23Stage 1 (IFRS): To be used if the instrument is not impaired and a loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected credit losses is raised against the instrument under IFRS. Only for instruments subject to impairment under IFRS 9.
24Stage 2 (IFRS): To be used if the instrument is not impaired and a loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected credit losses is raised against the instrument under IFRS. Only for instruments subject to impairment under IFRS 9.
25Stage 3 (IFRS): To be used if the instrument is impaired and a loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected credit losses is raised against the instrument under IFRS. Only for instruments subject to impairment under IFRS 9.
26Specific allowances (GAAP): To be used if the instrument is subject to impairment in accordance with an applied accounting standard other than IFRS 9 and specific loss allowances are raised, irrespective of whether these allowances are individually or collectively assessed (impaired
27Purchased or originated credit-impaired instruments (POCI) (IFRS)
INTL_IMPRMNT_STTS
Values List
ValueDescription
23Stage 1 (IFRS)
24Stage 2 (IFRS)
25Stage 3 (IFRS)
LNG_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_PRDNTL_PRTFL_ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTN_ASSGNMNT_ACCNTNG_STNDRD_TYP
Values List
ValueDescription
1Long balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment International Financial Reporting Standard (IFRS)
2Long balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP)

2Long balance sheet recognised equity or fund security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment position

Table NameLNG_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_PRDNTL_PRTFL_ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTN_ASSGNMNT_NGAAP_FDCRY_ITM
Functional NameLong balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP) fiduciary item
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionLong balance sheet recognised security position  prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment national general accepted accounting principles (nGAAP) fiduciary asset is a Long balance sheet recognised security position  prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment that is a fiduciary asset

Notes
Number Of Columns12
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTNPFYString (3) DOMAccounting classification for assets
2PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYPPFYString (1) DOMPrudential portfolio
3LNG_SHRT_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_TYPPFYStringDOMLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position type (Input Layer)
4RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
6INVSTR_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7INVSTR_ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
8DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
9ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
10SCRTY_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
11SCRTY_PSTN_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_TYPPFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
12FDCRYYString (1) DOMFiduciary asset indicator
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTNAccounting portfolio where the asset or liability is recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP –under Regulation (EU) 2015/534 (ECB/2015/13) applied by the observed agent's legal entity.
2PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between trading and banking (or non-trading) book.
4RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6INVSTR_PRTY_IDAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
7INVSTR_ENTTY_RL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
8DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
10SCRTY_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
12FDCRYIdentification of instruments in which the observed agent acts in its own name but on behalf of and with the risk borne by a third party.

Values List
ValueDescription
10 nGAAP: Loans and receivables
12nGAAP: Held-to-maturity investments
14IFRS: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with IFRS.
2IFRS: Financial assets held for trading: Financial assets held for trading in accordance with IFRS.
3nGAAP: Trading Financial assets: Trading financial assets in accordance with national GAAP.
4IFRS: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets measured at fair value through profit and loss and designated as such upon initial recognition or subsequently in accordance with IFRS, except those classified as financial assets held for trading.
41IFRS: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with IFRS.
45nGAAP: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits: Cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits in accordance with national GAAP.
46nGAAP: Financial assets held for trading
47nGAAP: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss: Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with national GAAP.
48nGAAP: Non-trading financial assets mandatorily at fair value through profit or loss
5nGAAP: Available-for-sale financial assets
6IFRS: Financial assets at amortised cost: Financial assets measured at amortised cost in accordance with IFRS.
64nGAAP: financial assets at fair value or strict LOCOM
7nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value through profit or loss: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.
711Accounting portfolios for financial assets other than classified as held for sale, excluding financial assets held for trading, trading financial assets and cash and cash balances at central banks and other demand deposits
73nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. LOCOM: nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets at LOCOM
74nGAAP: Other non-trading non-derivative financial assets. Other than LOCOM
76nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. LOCOM: nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method at LOCOM
77nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at a cost-based method. Other than LOCOM
8IFRS: Financial assets at fair value through other comprehensive income: Financial assets measured at fair value through other comprehensive income due to business model and cash-flows characteristics in accordance with IFRS.
83Investments in subsidiaries, joint ventures and associates.
851nGAAP: Accounting portfolios for trading financial assetss. Cost based method or LOCOM
9nGAAP: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity: Non-trading non-derivative financial assets measured at fair value to equity in accordance with national GAAP.

Table NameLNG_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_PRDNTL_PRTFL_ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTN_ASSGNMNT_TKN_PSSSN
Functional NameLong balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment taken into possession
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Long balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio Accounting classification assignment taken into possession is a  Long balance sheet recognised security position that was not pledged by the debtor as collateral, but was obtained in exchange for the cancellation of debt, whether on a voluntary basis or as part of legal proceeding
Notes
Number Of Columns16
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTNPFYString (3) DOMAccounting classification for assets
2PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYPPFYString (1) DOMPrudential portfolio
3LNG_SHRT_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_TYPPFYStringDOMLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position type (Input Layer)
4RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
6INVSTR_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7INVSTR_ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
8DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
9ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
10SCRTY_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
11SCRTY_PSTN_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_TYPPFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
12ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FV_EXPSR_CRYDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
13DRCGNSD_EXPSR_ACCMLTD_IMPRMNTYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
14GCA_EXPSR_DRCGNSD_EXCHNG_CLLTRLYDECIMAL (2)DOMPositive and negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
15GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNT_INTL_RCGNTNYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
16TKN_PSSSSN_DTYDateDOMAll possible dates
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1ACCNTNG_CLSSFCTNAccounting portfolio where the asset or liability is recorded in accordance with the accounting standard – IFRS or national GAAP –under Regulation (EU) 2015/534 (ECB/2015/13) applied by the observed agent's legal entity.
2PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between trading and banking (or non-trading) book.
4RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6INVSTR_PRTY_IDAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
7INVSTR_ENTTY_RL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
8DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
10SCRTY_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
12ACCMLTD_CHNGS_FV_EXPSR_CRAccumulated changes in fair value due to credit risk in accordance with Part 2.46 of Annex V to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 680/2014 of the exposure that is derecognised because of the asset obtained by taking possession.

Note: This amount must only be reported when it is negative.
13DRCGNSD_EXPSR_ACCMLTD_IMPRMNTAccumulated impairment is the amount of loss allowances that are held against or are allocated to the derecognised exposure on the reporting reference date for which the asset obtained by taking possession has been obtained.

This data attribute applies to assets subject to impairment under the applied accounting standard. Under IFRS, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to 12-month expected
credit losses;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to lifetime expected
credit losses.
Under GAAP, the accumulated impairment relates to the following
amounts:
(i) loss allowance at an amount equal to general allowances;
(ii) loss allowance at an amount equal to specific allowances.
15GRSS_CRRYNG_AMNT_INTL_RCGNTNGross carrying amount at the point in time of the initial recognition in the balance sheet 
16TKN_PSSSSN_DTBalance sheet recognition date is a date on the asset obtained by taking possession  was taken into possession and thus recognised on the balance sheet.

Abbreviation

MV Prebuilt
MV Query

Table NameLNG_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_PRDNTL_PRTFL_ASSGNMNT_RSK_DT
Functional NameLong balance sheet recognised security position prudential portfolio assignment risk data
Abbreviation
Classification Type Name
Object Type Name
MV Prebuilt
MV Query

DescriptionA Long balance sheet recognised security position risk data is risk related information about a Long balance sheet recognised debt security position.
Notes
Number Of Columns17
Number Of Rows Min.0
Number Of Rows Max.9999999
Expected Number Of Rows0
Expected Growth0
Growth IntervalYear
Used In Diagrams
Diagram Name
Input Layer 6.7
Columns
NoColumn NamePKFKMData TypeDT 
kind		Domain NameFormula
(Default Value)		SecurityAbbreviation
1EXPSR_CLSSPYString (2) DOMExposure classes for the calculation of risk weight
2PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYPPFYString (1) DOMPrudential portfolio
3LNG_SHRT_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_TYPPFYStringDOMLong/Short balance sheet recognised security position type (Input Layer)
4RPRTNG_AGNT_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLPFYString (1) DOMAccounting consolidation level (Input layer)
6INVSTR_PRTY_IDPFYString (120) DOMString up to 120 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
7INVSTR_ENTTY_RL_TYPPFYString (2) DOMParty role type (Input Layer)
8DT_RFRNCPFYDateDOMAll possible dates
9ACCNTNG_STNDRDPFYString (1) DOMAccounting framework (Input layer)
10SCRTY_IDPFYString (60) DOMString up to 60 characters limited to letters (capital and low cases), numbers, dash and underscore
11SCRTY_PSTN_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_TYPPFYStringDOMSecurity position Balance sheet recognised type (Input Layer)
12APPRCH_PRDNTL_PRPSSYString (3) DOMApproach for prudential purposes
13EXPSR_VLYDECIMALDOMNon-negative monetary amounts with 2 decimals
14LGD_DWNTRNSYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
15LGD_NRMLYDECIMALDOMNon-negative real numbers with 2 decimals
16RSK_WGHTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
17SPCFC_RSK_WGHTYDECIMALDOMReal numbers with 2 decimals
Columns Comments
NoColumn NameDescriptionNotes
1EXPSR_CLSSExposure class according to Regulation (EU) 575/2013, Article 112 Exposure class.
2PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between trading and banking (or non-trading) book.
4RPRTNG_AGNT_IDIdentifier of the reporting agent.
5ACCNTNG_CNSLDTN_LVLAccounting consolidation level is the level on which the accounting is consolidated, Solo or group.
6INVSTR_PRTY_IDAn identifier that uniquely identifies a party.
7INVSTR_ENTTY_RL_TYPA discriminator to distinguish between different roles a party may act in. Examples are debtor, creditor, servicer.
8DT_RFRNCThe date the data refers to.
10SCRTY_IDAn identifier to uniquely identify each security.
12APPRCH_PRDNTL_PRPSSIn accordance with Article 107 of the CRR, Approach to which Standardized Approach provided for in Chapter 2 or, if permitted by the competent authorities in accordance with Article 143, the Internal Ratings Based Approach provided for in Chapter 3 to calculate their risk-weighted exposure amounts for the purposes of points (a) and (f) of Article 92(3).
13EXPSR_VLExposure Value according to Regulation (EU) 575/2013, Article 111 Exposure value.
14LGD_DWNTRNSLoss given default (LGD) in downturns
15LGD_NRMLLoss given default (LGD) in normal economic times
16RSK_WGHTRisk-weight
17SPCFC_RSK_WGHTSpecific risk weight.
Indexes
Index NameStateFunctionalSpatialExpressionColumn NameSort
Order
LNG_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_SCRTY_PSTN_PRDNTL_PRTFL_ASSGNMNT_RSK_DT_PKPKEXPSR_CLSSASC

INVSTR_ENTTY_RL_TYPASC
DT_RFRNCASC
ACCNTNG_STNDRDASC
SCRTY_IDASC
SCRTY_PSTN_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_TYPASC
Constraints
TypeColumn / Constraint NameDetails
Column LevelEXPSR_CLSS
Values List
ValueDescription
1SA: Equity exposures
10SA: Exposures to international organisations
11SA: Exposures to multilateral development banks
12SA: Exposures to public sector entities
13SA: Exposures to regional governments or local authorities
14SA: Items associated with a particular high risk
16SA: Other items
2SA: Exposures in default
3SA: Exposures in the form of covered bonds
4SA: Exposures in the form of units or shares in CIUs
6SA: Exposures to central governments or central banks
7SA: Exposures to corporates without a short-term credit assessment
8SA: Exposures to institutions and corporates with a short-term credit assessment
9SA: Exposures to institutions without a short-term credit assessment
PRDNTL_PRTFL_TYP
Values List
ValueDescription
1Trading book
2Non-trading book

ValueDescription
0Not applicable
1Solo consolidation level
2Group consolidation level

0Not applicable
1National GAAP not consistent with IFRS
2IFRS
3National GAAP consistent with IFRS
SCRTY_PSTN_BLNC_SHT_RCGNSD_TYP
Values List
ValueDescription
1Balance sheet recognised security position
3Non-balance sheet recognised debt security position
4Non-balance sheet recognised equity or fund security position
APPRCH_PRDNTL_PRPSS
Values List
ValueDescription
11250% for positions not subject to any method: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions to which a risk weight of 1250% is assigned in accordance with Article 259(1)(d) of CRR.
118SEC-IRBA: Internal ratings-based approach for securitisation positions: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the Internal ratings-based approach is used.
122SEC-SA: Standardised approach for securitisations: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the Standardised approach is used.
125SEC-ERBA: External ratings-based approach for securitisation positions: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the External ratings-based approach is used
18Ratings based method: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the Ratings based method set out in Article 261 of CRR is used.
19Supervisory formula method: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the Supervisory formula method set out in Article 262 of CRR is used.
25Internal assessment approach: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the Internal assessment approach set out in Article 259(3) and (4) of CRR is used.
30Look-through approach: Securitisations: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for securitisation positions for which the Look-through approach set out in Article 259(1)(e) of CRR is used.
33IRB: Internal models approach: Equity exposures: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for equity exposures for which the Internal models approach set out in Article 155(4) of CRR is used.
34IRB: PD/LGD approach: Equity exposures: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for equity exposures for which the PD/LGD approach set out in Article 155(3) of CRR is used.
35IRB: Simple risk weight approach: Equity exposures: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for equity exposures for which the Simple risk weight approach set out in Article 155(2) of CRR is used.
42Standardised approach: Standardised approach to calculate the risk-weighted exposure amounts in accordance with Chapter 2 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013
66Advanced IRB: Internal Ratings Based (IRB) approach to calculate the risk-weighted exposure amounts in accordance with Chapter 3 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013 and banks calculate and use, in addition to the PDs, their own risk parameter subject to the supervisory guid
67Foundation IRB: Internal Ratings Based (IRB) approach to calculate the risk-weighted exposure amounts in accordance with Chapter 3 of Regulation (EU) No 575/2013 and banks use their own-calculated PDs, while for the other parameters (such as LGD) predefined values provid
68IRB: Fixed risk weights: Equity exposures: Calculation of the risk-weighted exposure amounts for equity exposures which attract a fixed risk weight treatment (without however being explicitly treated according to the Simple Risk Weight approach or the (temporary or permanent) partial use of the credit risk standardised approach) set out in Article 155 of CRR.
LGD_DWNTRNS
Ranges
Begin ValueEnd ValueDescription
0
RSK_WGHT
Ranges
Begin ValueEnd ValueDescription
0
SPCFC_RSK_WGHT
Ranges
Begin ValueEnd ValueDescription
0
